evidence.display: 39 y/o M with ETOH abuse who presented on [**3-18**] with lactic acidosis and
evidence.text: 39 y/o M with ETOH abuse who presented on [**3-18**] with lactic acidosis and    profound weakness.

evidence.display: He was found to be severely thiamine deficient    consistent with a diagnosis of
evidence.text: He was found to be severely thiamine deficient    consistent with a diagnosis of Beri Beri.

evidence.display: Pt was repleted aggressively    with Thiamine, MIV and had an NG placed for nutr
evidence.text: Pt was repleted aggressively    with Thiamine, MIV and had an NG placed for nutrition.

evidence.display: He developed    re-feeding syndrome requiring aggressive electrolyte repletion.
evidence.text: He developed    re-feeding syndrome requiring aggressive electrolyte repletion.

evidence.display: He    developed ETOH withdrawal and mental status changes approximately    24-48
evidence.text: He    developed ETOH withdrawal and mental status changes approximately    24-48hr after admission.

evidence.display: He was treated with small doses of Valium and    mental status improved somewhat
evidence.text: He was treated with small doses of Valium and    mental status improved somewhat before he was called out to medicine on    [**3-21**].

evidence.display: On [**3-23**], he was advanced a dysphagia diet and the following day, he    spi
evidence.text: On [**3-23**], he was advanced a dysphagia diet and the following day, he    spiked a fever to 101.8.

evidence.display: Initial infectious work up revealed negative    UA, neg Blood Cx, negative Cdiff
evidence.text: Initial infectious work up revealed negative    UA, neg Blood Cx, negative Cdiff and possible infiltrate on CXR.

evidence.display: Chest    CT revealed bilateral pleural effusions and atelectasis.
evidence.text: Chest    CT revealed bilateral pleural effusions and atelectasis.

evidence.display: He triggered on [**2191-3-24**] for tachypnea, tachycardia, and hypoxia.
evidence.text: He triggered on [**2191-3-24**] for tachypnea, tachycardia, and hypoxia.

evidence.display: His    hypoxia resolved, and CTA was subsequently negative for PE.
evidence.text: His    hypoxia resolved, and CTA was subsequently negative for PE.

evidence.display: Mental    status further deteriorated after fevers began and there was concern
evidence.text: Mental    status further deteriorated after fevers began and there was concern    for intermittent seizures due to bilateral upper extremity tremors and    periodic eye deviation.  2mg Ativan given without significant    improvement.

evidence.display: Pt was loaded with Keppra 1gram, started on Acyclovir    700mg IV TID and Keppra
evidence.text: Pt was loaded with Keppra 1gram, started on Acyclovir    700mg IV TID and Keppra 1500mg [**Hospital1 7**].

evidence.display: Neuro attending recommended    transfer to MICU for continuous EEG monitoring.
evidence.text: Neuro attending recommended    transfer to MICU for continuous EEG monitoring.

evidence.display: Upon arrival to MICU, he was transported pt to head ct which was + for    small
evidence.text: Upon arrival to MICU, he was transported pt to head ct which was + for    small 4 mm subdural.

evidence.display: Throughout the night, ? r/t keppra dosing pt    became more somnulent, acidotic
evidence.text: Throughout the night, ? r/t keppra dosing pt    became more somnulent, acidotic and o2 dependent.

evidence.display: He was intubated then    ([**3-26**]) for airway protection.    .H/O airway, Ina
evidence.text: He was intubated then    ([**3-26**]) for airway protection.    .H/O airway, Inability to Protect (Risk for Aspiration, Altered Gag,    Airway Clearance, Cough)    Assessment:    Suctioning ETT for scant white sputum.
Lung sounds rhonchorus.

evidence.display: Probably able to protect his own airway    now.
evidence.text: Probably able to protect his own airway    now.
Plan:    Monitor mental status, ?extubation today.

evidence.text: Altered mental status (not Delirium)    Assessment:    No seizure activity noted.
